skill.name: documentation-templates
skill.description: Documentation templates and structure guidelines. README, API docs, code comments, and AI-friendly documentation.
skill.summary: Templates for README, API docs, code comments, changelogs, and AI-friendly documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 23 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation templates with no executable code. The detected patterns (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithms, system reconnaissance) are markdown code fences, plain English text, and API documentation placeholders - not actual security risks.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected backticks as shell command execution. These are markdown code block fences (```), not actual shell commands. The skill contains only documentation templates.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All detected locations are markdown code blocks using triple backticks (```) as fences, not shell backticks for command substitution. This is a documentation-only skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected plain English words matching cryptographic patterns. These are documentation text, not actual cryptographic code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The analyzer matched words like 'Default' in table headers and generic documentation text. No cryptographic algorithms are used - this is purely a documentation template file.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected API endpoint templates and documentation placeholders as system reconnaissance. These are documentation patterns, not actual reconnaissance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 68 is an API template 'GET /users/:id', line 147 is ADR template '## Decision', line 180 mentions 'Mermaid diagrams'. These are documentation placeholders, not system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: This skill provides ready-to-use templates for README files, API documentation, code comments, changelogs, and AI-friendly documentation formats.
actual_capabilities: Generate README.md files with essential sections in priority order
Create API documentation with endpoint, parameters, and response templates
Format changelogs following Keep a Changelog standard
limitations: This skill provides templates only, not executable code generation
Templates require manual adaptation to specific project needs
Does not validate or generate actual code, only documentation structure
No integration with external documentation tools

use_cases.title: New Project Setup
use_cases.description: Quickly scaffold documentation for a new project with README, API docs, and changelog templates.

use_cases.title: Documentation Standards
use_cases.description: Maintain consistent documentation style across team projects using standardized templates.

use_cases.title: AI Agent Integration
use_cases.description: Create documentation that AI agents like Claude and Codex can understand and use effectively.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic README
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create a README.md for my [project type] project. Include Quick Start, Features, and Configuration sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick README for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Document API Endpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create API documentation for my REST API with endpoints for [list endpoints].

prompt_templates.title: Write Code Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to add JSDoc comments to my function that [describe function purpose].

prompt_templates.title: Create Architecture Decision Record
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the documentation-templates skill to create an ADR for [decision title]. Include Context, Decision, and Consequences sections.

output_examples.input: Use documentation-templates to create a README for a Node.js API
output_examples.output: A complete README.md with sections: Title + One-liner, Quick Start, Features, Configuration table, API Reference link, Contributing guidelines, License

output_examples.input: Use documentation-templates to document a GET /users endpoint
output_examples.output: API documentation with endpoint description, Parameters table (id: string, required), Response codes (200, 404), and example request/response

output_examples.input: Use documentation-templates to create an ADR for database choice
output_examples.output: ADR document with Status (Accepted/Deprecated/Superseded), Context (why the decision is needed), Decision (what was decided), Consequences (trade-offs)

best_practices: Start with essential sections first (title, quick start, features) before adding detailed documentation
Use templates as starting points and adapt the structure to your project needs
Keep documentation up to date - outdated docs are worse than no documentation
Include examples in your documentation to help users understand quickly
anti_patterns: Do not copy templates verbatim without customizing to your project context
Avoid documenting implementation details in user-facing README files
Do not skip the Quick Start section - users want to get started fast
Avoid using outdated documentation formats that AI agents cannot parse

faq.question: What tools is this skill compatible with?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Use it with the Read, Glob, and Grep tools.

faq.question: Do I need to modify the templates?
faq.answer: Yes, templates are starting points. Adapt the placeholders and structure to fit your specific project needs.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is licensed under MIT, allowing commercial use with attribution.

faq.question: What is llms.txt?
faq.answer: llms.txt is a documentation format designed for AI crawlers and agents. It provides a structured overview that AI models can easily parse and understand.

faq.question: How is this different from auto-generated docs?
faq.answer: This skill provides human-written templates that prioritize clarity and scannability. Auto-generated docs often lack proper structure and context.

faq.question: Can this skill generate actual code?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates documentation structures and templates. It does not produce executable code.
